mods.abstract: Pages 204-221: a fragmentary stele found near Turfan, which dates to the reign of Wu Zhao 武瞾 (r. 690–705). The original stele was broken into 10 pieces, 1 large piece, nine smaller. Only a rubbing of the large fragment has been published. Rong gives a transcription, which he amends a bit with transmitted transcriptions of the text carved on the nine small fragments. The first part of the text is a list of sutra titles, the second part is a colophon. Rong identifies the sutra titles. Among these, the most recently translated text (693) is that of the Baoyu jing 寳雨經. Therfore, the stele text can be dated to 693-705 (Wu Zetian characters). The stele fragment has been termed 武周康居士寫經功德記碑. Rong believes that it was the intention of the donor, Kang jushi 康居士, to actually copy all of the texts indicated in the catalog, yet this catalog includes the 600 scrolls of T#220. Therefore, Claudia thinks that the list of sutras, or the catalog, is just an abbreviation for the entire Buddhist canon. A pdf of this article is on our server.

name.namePart: 生活·讀書·新知三聯書店
